objective: Implement the independently verifiable F-0096 paper_attachment_usage integrity partition without claiming finding closure: register the six required purposes (paper file, material paper, answer sheet, answer paper, analysis file, source material), preserve existing other rows as legacy read-only compatibility, reject new other uploads, and let content administrators explicitly choose and view the supported purpose. Generate only offline Drizzle migration source; do not execute migration or access a real database. Do not change upload size/type/streaming, storage, download authorization, physical deletion, outbox, reference counting, dependencies or permissions.
blockedFiles: migration execution, persistent database access or real data
local storage, download authorization, physical deletion, outbox or reference counting
role, permission, entitlement or learner visibility changes
dependency, Provider, external service, secret/env, deployment, PR or force-push
F-0097, F-0098 or another independent finding

authoritativeChainEvidence.requirements: Module 02 section 3.9 defines six file purposes and requires each attachment to carry its purpose.
authoritativeChainEvidence.glossary: glossary.yaml is the enum naming SSOT and currently lists only paper_source, answer_analysis, answer_sheet and other.
authoritativeChainEvidence.schemaMigration: paperAttachmentUsageValues owns the PostgreSQL enum consumed by paper_asset and paper_asset_upload_operation; additive enum migration source is required, with no database execution.
authoritativeChainEvidence.runtimeValidator: The multipart runtime and service validator consume the enum before upload preparation and persistence; supported create values must be the six required purposes while legacy other remains readable.
authoritativeChainEvidence.uiCaller: AdminPaperManagementClient currently hard-codes every upload to paper_source; AdminPaperDetailPage and the direct AdminPaperManagement fixture own purpose labels for existing rows.
authoritativeChainEvidence.scopeAmendment: guardian-f0096-paper-asset-usage-contract-scope-amendment-01-2026-07-21 permits only seven-value read labels in the direct fixture and a creatable-usage input type in the direct local storage adapter; no storage behavior changes.
authoritativeChainEvidence.tests: Schema, service, runtime, storage adapter, saga and admin UI tests are the direct contracts and mocks.
authoritativeChainEvidence.nonClaims: The prior b4b21d527 partition already added private download and draft-only metadata delete. This task does not address streaming gates or durable physical file lifecycle, so F-0096 stays open.

scopeAmendments.semanticBoundary: read-label completeness and creatable input type narrowing only

validationEvidence.tddRed: 4 test files; 34 tests; 6 expected contract failures; exit 1
validationEvidence.lint: 8 affected product files including both amended direct-chain files; exit 0
validationEvidence.typecheck: tsc --noEmit; exit 0 after the exact scope amendment
validationEvidence.build: Next.js production build; 100 routes; exit 0; existing NFT trace warning only
validationEvidence.formatAndDiff: targeted Prettier check and git diff --check pass
validationEvidence.schemaSource: snapshot drift is limited to paper_attachment_usage enum values; migration contains exactly three additive ADD VALUE statements and no destructive or data SQL
validationEvidence.adversarialReview: pass: seven persisted/read values and six creatable values remain distinct; legacy other remains list/detail/download compatible and is rejected before upload file I/O; no cast/default fallback, storage behavior, permission, dependency, real database or extra-file drift

resumeAnchor.condition: b4b21d527 secure-read and draft-retention partition is closed; the usage contract candidate is validated and adversarially reviewed under the guardian parent approval plus scope amendment; perform only conditional closeout without reopening download or retention scope
